title: Family Medicine and Palliative Care
convener: Doctor N Beckett (Year 4& 6 FM UG Convenor), Doctor H Salie (Year 6 FM Co- Convenor), Doctor M De Swardt (Year 4&6 Palliative Care Co-Convenor)

prerequisites.text: Successful completion of all co urses within the preceding academic year.

outline: Objective: A: Cognitive Domain: By the end of the 4-week clerkship, the students should demonstrate the ability: (a) To apply & integrat e the principles of FM, PC & PHC in a consultation, (b) To formulate a 3 -stage assessment using the SOAP format, (c) To critically appraise and apply evidence from scientiﬁc journals to patient care, (d) To assess family structure and resources using a Genogram, (e) To comply with ethical and legal rules when issuing medical certi ﬁcates, (f) To understand and apply the guidelines for completion of disability grant applications, (g) To show an understanding and apply the various health promotio n models, (h) To recognize chil d abuse and domestic/IPV in a consultation, (i) To understand the medico-legal is sues and ethical practice in family medicine and palliative medicine. B: Psychomotor Domain: By the end of the 4-week clerkship the students should be able: (a) To demonstrate the skills of behaviour change counselling to address identiﬁed risk factors in a clinical consultation, (b) To demonstrate the skill of breaking bad news to a patient in a distressing situation, and to communicate effect ively with a terminal patient and their fam ilies, (c) To counsel and manage a dying patient and fa mily members. Manage grief and loss, (d) To perform basic procedural skills outlined in logboo k of procedural skills, (e) To take a histor y, diagnose & manage chronic diseases of lifestyle in the community, (f) To diagnose and manage undifferentiated conditions, (g) To diagnos e and manage HIV at primary level, (h) Must recognize reversible conditions in life- threatening illn ess. C: Affective Domain: The st udent should be ab le to: (a) To demonstrate a patient-centred approach in a consultation, including empathy and professionalism, (b) To demonstrate reﬂective practice in a consultation, (c) To function effectively in a multidisciplinary team, (d) To understand ethics/ethical practice relevant to Family Medicine & Palliative Medicine Course outline: The clerkship aims to provide students with the necessary confidence and competence to enter the six- month family medicine internship, as well as a basis for postgraduate training in the practice of family medicine and palliative care. During the 4-week rotation, all students based in Cape Town will spend two weeks at community health centres (CHCs) within the district health system in the Cape Town metropolitan area, followed by a two-week rura l rotation in Vr edenburg, within th e rural district health services in the Western Cape. Palliative care learning focuses on clinical aspects such as pain management and introduces paediatric palliative care. Palliative care activities may include visits to a hospice, patient’s home, intermediate care facility, paediatric health care facilities and district hospital exposure.
lecture_times: See module block schedule and official class timetable.
dp_requirements: (a) Completion of all required coursework and attendance of compulsory academic activities on campus (including orientation day session and tutorials/seminars conducted virtually or DEPARTMENTS IN THE FACULTY 83 face to face). A completed Portfolio and a completed Logbook must be submitted on Amathuba as a DP requirement. (b) Attendance of all clinical act ivities at the community health clinics (CHC), palliative care sites and Vredenburg. Any missed FM clinical activities must be made up to meet DP, and each student must have a minimum of 5 observed Mini-Cex consultations at the Metro sites. The limited time at the Metro and VB sites makes it challenging to make up the missed clinical time during the 2 weeks. Any student who is un able to make up the missed cl inical time during the block must make up the missed time during the remaining academic year to meet the DP requirements. The student's mark will reflect as O/S on the transcript until the outstanding clinical time is made up and the EOB exam is completed. Once DP is met, the student is eligible to join any of the remaining End of Block OSCEs during the academic year, provided there are no clashes with other EOB exams. The O/S mark on the transcript will be changed to reflect the final FM mark. This is subject to an approved concession to miss classes application. Any missed palliative care clinical sessions must be made up by arrangement with the PC UG convenor. A valid medical certificate with approved DP concession to miss classes application must be submitted when FM and/or PC activities are missed. Any student who missed more than 25% of clinical activities will be DPR and will have failed FM. (c) Attendance at one SHAWCO session during the family medicine course/clerkship, unless otherwise indicated by the convenor.
assessment: The final mark is made up of: (A) an in-course mark comprising a patient study (25%) and a Facility Clinical mark (CHC and Vredenburg/Garden Route):15%. The facility clinical mark is weighted according to time spent at CHCs (50%) and VB (50%). The breakdown for the CHC facility mark and the VB rural facility mark is as follows: Professionalism:25%; Clinical: 25%; Attendance: 25%; Communication: 25%. A standardised rubric is used for the Facility Mark. The Mini-Cex work- based assessment is formative. The Portfolio for the 2x2 rotation is a form ative assessment which provides evidence of student engagement during the 2x2 rotati on in the Metro and Vredenburg. The portfolio will inform the Facility Clinical mark. For the Garden Rout e (GDR) students, the Facility Clinical Mark will consist of a Portfolio mark and a Global Facility Mark (B) OSCE (60%). A penalty of 5% per day will be deducted from the patient study component fo r late submissions of patient studies to a maximum of five days, following whi ch a mark of zero will be allocated. Any student who achieves more than 48% but less than 50% for either the F acility Clinical mark or the OSCE or both will be eligible for a supplementary examination at the discretion of the FEC Subcommittee. This may be associated with additional clin ical time at the discretion of the course convenor. A student who achieves less than 50% for the supplementary examination mu st repeat the entire 4-week FM rotation.
